meaning: n.<美口>(自以为)有高度文化修养的人,知识分子,卖弄知识的人adj.自炫博学的,知识分子的,不切实际的
examples: Language has been enriched with such terms as "highbrow", "egghead", "blue-stocking", "wiseacre", "know-all", "boffin" and, for many, "intellectual" is a term of denigration.
Highbrow critics sniff that the programme was "too sophisticated" to appeal to most viewers.
But highbrow films now come with higher hopes.
This is still NPR: safely highbrow, never snarky and, in its quest for inclusiveness, sometimes labored.
More than direct profit and loss, sponsorships of highbrow sporting events are about brand building.
